 {"id":"xf-53","title":"Task: Update cmd_stats to use get_all_counts","description":"## Purpose
Replace N+1 individual count queries in cmd_stats with the new batch method.

## Current Code Location
src/main.rs, cmd_stats function

## Changes Required
1. Replace individual get_*_count() calls with single get_all_counts() call
2. Use AllCounts struct fields instead of separate variables
3. Preserve existing output format exactly (text and JSON)
4. Ensure --detailed, --temporal, --engagement flags still work

## Before (Simplified)
```rust
let tweets = storage.get_tweet_count()?;
let likes = storage.get_like_count()?;
let dms = storage.get_dm_count()?;
// ... 7 more queries
```

## After
```rust
let counts = storage.get_all_counts()?;
// Use counts.tweets, counts.likes, counts.dms, etc.
```

## Testing Requirements
- Run xf stats before and after, verify identical output
- Run xf stats --detailed, verify identical output
- Run xf stats -f json, verify identical JSON structure
- Run xf stats -f json --detailed, verify identical output

## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is byte-for-byte identical to before (isomorphism)
- Single database call instead of 10+
- All existing stats tests pass
- No new warnings or errors","notes":"Review update (2026-01-12):
- Tests in xf-54 must confirm text/JSON output unchanged; add regression fixture if needed.
- E2E script logs stats timing and output snapshot.

Additional validation:
- Unit tests: output parity for text/json (xf-54).
- E2E: xf-57 logs stats timing + output snapshot.

Test plan: unit tests + e2e coverage with detailed logging (xf-57).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:54:33.27134499-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T11:11:29.787509309-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T11:11:29.787509309-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented (cmd_stats uses get_all_counts)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-53","depends_on_id":"xf-52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.659575301-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"xf-54","title":"Task: Unit tests for stats consolidation (xf-38)","description":"## Purpose
Verify get_all_counts method works correctly and cmd_stats output is unchanged.

## Test Cases Required

### 1. get_all_counts unit tests
- Returns correct counts for empty database
- Returns correct counts for database with mixed data
- Handles NULL date ranges gracefully
- Performance is better than N individual queries

### 2. Isomorphism tests
- cmd_stats output before == cmd_stats output after (text format)
- cmd_stats output before == cmd_stats output after (JSON format)
- cmd_stats --detailed output unchanged
- cmd_stats --temporal output unchanged

### 3. Edge case tests
- Database with only tweets (other counts = 0)
- Database with only DMs
- Very large counts (> 100K)
- Unicode in text doesn't affect counts

### Implementation Notes
- Create test database fixtures with known counts
- Use snapshot testing or golden files for output comparison
- Log timing comparison for performance verification

### Acceptance Criteria
- All unit tests pass
- Isomorphism verified (identical outputs)
- Performance improvement logged and verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:54:41.385508506-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T11:20:56.832705468-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T11:20:56.832705468-05:00","close_reason":"Added get_all_counts edge-case tests + stats JSON/text output assertions; remaining large-count/perf coverage tracked in xf-1nu","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-54","depends_on_id":"xf-53","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.661897527-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"xf-55","title":"Task: Refactor rrf_fuse to use references instead of clones","description":"## Purpose
Eliminate unnecessary heap allocations in RRF fusion by using references and borrowing.

## Current Problem (src/hybrid.rs:114-182)
The rrf_fuse function clones several data structures unnecessarily:
- line 133: lexical_results.insert(hit.id.clone(), hit.clone())
- line 134: doc_types.insert(hit.id.clone(), hit.result_type.to_string())
- line 153: doc_types.get(&doc_id).cloned()
- line 154: lexical_results.remove(&doc_id)

For 100 results, this creates ~400 unnecessary heap allocations.

## Solution Approach
Use lifetime parameters and references:

```rust
pub fn rrf_fuse<'a>(
    lexical: &'a [SearchResult],
    semantic: &[VectorSearchResult],
    limit: usize,
    offset: usize,
) -> Vec<FusedHit<'a>> {
    // Store indices instead of clones
    let mut lexical_indices: HashMap<&str, usize> = HashMap::new();
    // Reference original data instead of cloning
}
```

## Changes Required
1. Add lifetime parameter to rrf_fuse function signature
2. Change FusedHit.lexical from Option<SearchResult> to Option<&'a SearchResult>
3. Use &str keys in HashMaps instead of String
4. Store indices into lexical slice instead of clones
5. Update callers to handle new lifetime requirements

## Considerations
- This is a medium-complexity refactor due to lifetime propagation
- May require changes to FusedHit struct definition
- Must maintain exact same output (isomorphism)
- Benchmark before/after to verify improvement

## Acceptance Criteria
- No .clone() calls on SearchResult in rrf_fuse
- All existing hybrid search tests pass
- Output is identical to before (isomorphism)
- Measurable reduction in allocations (use heaptrack or similar)","notes":"Review update (2026-01-12):
- Tests in xf-56 must prove identical ordering/output; add a small perf check.
- E2E logs should include before/after RRF ordering for a fixed query.

Additional validation:
- Unit tests: ordering/score parity (xf-56).
- E2E: xf-57 logs before/after RRF ordering.

Test plan: unit tests + e2e coverage with detailed logging (xf-57).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:55:00.300120053-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T11:21:33.333579674-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T11:21:33.333579674-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented with xf-39 (rrf_fuse now uses borrowed doc_id/doc_type + lexical rank; no SearchResult clones)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-55","depends_on_id":"xf-39","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.664448013-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"xf-56","title":"Task: Unit tests for RRF clone elimination (xf-39)","description":"## Purpose
Verify RRF fusion refactor produces identical results while reducing allocations.

## Test Cases Required

### 1. Isomorphism tests (CRITICAL)
- rrf_fuse_new() produces exact same output as rrf_fuse_old() for all test inputs
- Ordering of results is identical
- Scores are identical (f32 bit-exact comparison)
- FusedHit fields match exactly

### 2. Existing test preservation
- test_rrf_basic still passes
- test_rrf_scoring still passes
- test_rrf_single_source still passes
- test_rrf_limit still passes
- test_rrf_offset still passes
- test_rrf_empty still passes
- test_rrf_zero_limit still passes
- test_rrf_deterministic still passes
- test_rrf_both_bonus still passes

### 3. Lifetime/borrow tests
- References remain valid for duration of use
- No use-after-free (compile-time guarantee)
- Returned FusedHit can be used after input slices are still alive

### 4. Performance verification
- Allocation count reduced (use #[global_allocator] counting)
- Throughput same or better for typical workloads

### Implementation Notes
- Run existing tests first, they should all pass
- Add allocation counting test if possible
- Use criterion benchmarks for performance comparison

### Acceptance Criteria
- All 9 existing RRF tests pass unchanged
- Isomorphism verified with property-based testing
- No new clippy warnings
- Performance not degraded","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:55:10.944336758-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:55:10.944336758-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-56","depends_on_id":"xf-55","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.666936142-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"xf-57","title":"Task: Create E2E performance validation test script","description":"## Purpose
Create a comprehensive end-to-end test script that validates all performance optimizations work correctly and produce identical outputs to the baseline.

## Script Location
tests/e2e_performance_validation.sh (or .rs if using Rust)

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Index Performance Test
```bash
# Time full index operation
time xf index /path/to/test-archive --force
# Expected: < 2 minutes for 50K docs with embeddings
```

### 2. Search Latency Tests
```bash
# Hybrid search (default)
time xf search 'test query' --limit 100
# Expected: < 50ms

# Lexical only
time xf search 'test query' --mode lexical --limit 100
# Expected: < 20ms

# Semantic only
time xf search 'test query' --mode semantic --limit 100
# Expected: < 30ms
```

### 3. Stats Performance Test
```bash
time xf stats
# Expected: < 100ms with consolidated queries
```

### 4. Isomorphism Verification
```bash
# Run same searches, compare outputs
xf search 'machine learning' -f json > output_new.json
# Compare with baseline output
diff -q output_new.json output_baseline.json || echo 'ISOMORPHISM FAILURE'
```

### 5. Memory Usage Test
```bash
# Monitor peak RSS during operations
/usr/bin/time -v xf search 'test' --limit 1000
# Expected: < 200MB peak for 100K doc index
```

### 6. Repeated Search Test (Cache Effectiveness)
```bash
# First search (cold)
time xf search 'query1'
# Second search (warm - embeddings cached)
time xf search 'query2'
# Expected: Second search significantly faster (no embedding reload)
```

## Output Format
Script should produce structured output:
```
=== XF Performance Validation ===
Date: 2026-01-12
Commit: abc123

[PASS] Index performance: 45s (target: <120s)
[PASS] Hybrid search latency: 32ms (target: <50ms)
[PASS] Stats latency: 45ms (target: <100ms)
[PASS] Isomorphism check: All outputs match baseline
[PASS] Memory usage: 145MB peak (target: <200MB)
[PASS] Cache effectiveness: 2nd search 85% faster

Overall: 6/6 PASSED
```

## Prerequisites
- Test archive with known data (use standardized corpus from xf-47)
- Baseline outputs captured before optimization
- hyperfine or similar for accurate timing

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script runs without manual intervention
- Clear PASS/FAIL output for each test
- Exit code 0 only if all tests pass
- Timing data logged for trend analysis
- Can be run in CI pipeline","notes":"Review update (2026-01-12):
- E2E script must log: command + args, environment (XF_DB/XF_INDEX), dataset size, start/end timestamps, duration, exit code, stdout/stderr, and result counts.
- Save logs to a deterministic path and include diffs vs baseline in docs/performance_baseline.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:55:34.499903991-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T05:41:20.005580108-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T05:41:20.005580108-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-57","depends_on_id":"xf-47","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.669328359-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"xf-59","title":"Task: Unit tests for N+1 replacement (xf-43)","description":"## Purpose
Verify the N+1 loop replacement produces identical results and improves performance.

## Test Cases Required

### 1. Isomorphism Tests (CRITICAL)
- `test_batch_lookup_same_as_individual`
  - Run search with N+1 pattern, capture results
  - Run search with batch pattern, capture results
  - Assert results are identical (order, content, scores)

- `test_batch_lookup_preserves_result_order`
  - Semantic search returns IDs in score order
  - Batch lookup must return in same order
  - Verify order matches for various queries

### 2. Edge Case Tests
- `test_batch_lookup_with_empty_results`
  - Semantic search returns no results
  - Batch lookup handles gracefully
  
- `test_batch_lookup_with_missing_docs`
  - Some doc IDs don't exist in Tantivy (deleted?)
  - Batch lookup omits missing, doesn't crash

- `test_batch_lookup_with_type_filter`
  - Filter by doc_type works with batch lookup
  - Only matching types returned

### 3. Performance Tests
- `test_batch_faster_than_loop`
  - Time 100 individual get_by_id calls
  - Time 1 get_by_ids call with 100 IDs
  - Assert batch is significantly faster (>5x)
  - Log timing comparison

### 4. Integration Tests
- `test_hybrid_search_uses_batch_lookup`
  - Run hybrid search
  - Verify results are correct
  - Verify performance is within target

## Implementation Location
`src/search.rs` test module or `tests/batch_lookup.rs`

## Logging Requirements
- Log timing comparison (individual vs batch)
- Log number of IDs processed
- Log any missing document IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Isomorphism verified (identical outputs)
- [ ] Edge cases handled correctly
- [ ] Performance improvement measured (>5x faster)
- [ ] All existing search tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:17:07.79837819-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:17:07.79837819-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-59","depends_on_id":"xf-43","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.673022701-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"xf-9rf.3","title":"Random deep-dive file investigations + flow traces","description":"Goal:
- Randomly sample code files and deeply trace execution flows through imports/callers to build intuitive understanding beyond the main path.

Steps:
1) Use rg/rg --files to list candidate files; pick a random subset across modules (parser, search, storage, CLI, tests, benches).
2) For each file: identify entry points, key data structures, and how its functions are called from other modules.
3) Trace at least 2 multi-hop flows (file A -> file B -> file C) with notes on inputs/outputs and invariants.
4) Record any surprising behavior or potential correctness pitfalls discovered during tracing.

Acceptance:
- At least 5 files deeply analyzed with call/flow notes.
- Flow traces identify caller/callee relationships and data transformations across module boundaries.
","notes":"Deep-dive notes (random file sampling + flow traces):
- src/main.rs: command dispatcher; cmd_index -> ArchiveParser::parse_* -> Storage::store_* + SearchEngine::index_* -> writer.commit + reload. cmd_search -> SearchEngine::search -> output formatting; cmd_stats/tweet/list/export/config/update are stubs/partial (yellow warnings) in main.
- src/parser.rs: read_data_file -> parse_js_file (split on first '=') -> serde_json Value -> parse_* into model structs. Uses rayon par_iter for JSON arrays. parse_manifest reads manifest.js and normalizes dates/numeric fields.
- src/search.rs: schema defines id/text/text_prefix/type/created_at/metadata. index_* builds Tantivy docs; search() builds QueryParser over text+text_prefix, optional type filters with BooleanQuery; SnippetGenerator for highlights; SearchResult assembled with metadata JSON.
- src/storage.rs: opens SQLite with WAL + perf pragmas; migrate/create schema; store_* inserts per model and maintains FTS5 tables; get_tweet/stats queries. Data normalized; DM messages linked by conversation_id.
- src/cli.rs: defines flags (types, limit/offset/sort, since/until, replies/context/fields). Several flags are not wired in main.rs yet (potential correctness/UX gap for later bug-hunt).
- src/config.rs/logging.rs/perf.rs/error.rs: full-featured config/logging/perf budgets/custom errors defined but currently not integrated into main.rs (latent functionality).
- benches/search_perf.rs: synthetic benchmarks invoke SearchEngine and Storage directly; provides baseline for search/index/storage; uses TempDir and synthetic models.

Multi-hop flow traces:
1) xf index -> main.rs cmd_index -> ArchiveParser::parse_tweets -> model::Tweet -> Storage::store_tweets (SQLite inserts + FTS5) -> SearchEngine::index_tweets (Tantivy doc) -> commit + reload.
2) xf search -> main.rs cmd_search -> SearchEngine::search -> QueryParser + TopDocs -> SnippetGenerator -> model::SearchResult -> main.rs output formatter (json/csv/text/compact).

Potential pitfalls observed (for later bug review):
- CLI flags defined but not used (sort/since/until/replies/context/fields/threads etc.).
- Config/logging/perf modules unused; custom error types largely bypassed by anyhow usage in main.

 {"id":"xf-9rf.4","title":"Review prior agent changes for correctness and regressions","description":"Goal:
- Audit code written by other agents (not limited to latest commits) to identify bugs, inefficiencies, or regressions.

Steps:
1) Use git log/diff to locate code changes authored by agents; scan both recent and older edits.
2) For each change: verify logic vs requirements; look for edge cases, incorrect assumptions, or performance pitfalls.
3) Cross-check with tests/benches; add targeted tests if a bug is found.
4) Record root-cause analysis for any issues discovered.

Acceptance:
- A documented list of reviewed changes, with issues (if any) and their root causes.
- Any fixes are minimal, scoped, and comply with AGENTS.md editing rules.
","notes":"Reviewed uncommitted/agent-origin diffs:
- src/main.rs: cmd_export implemented (JSON/JSONL/CSV) + csv_escape/format_export helpers; uses Storage::get_all_* and writes to file or stdout. No correctness regressions spotted; minor risk: CSV header order uses serde_json map iteration; should be deterministic for struct field order.
- src/storage.rs: new get_all_tweets/likes/dms/followers/following; migrate now takes &self; json fields parsed with serde_json::from_str (stored values are always serialized JSON, so NULL risk low). get_all_dms drops conversation_id because DirectMessage model lacks it.
- src/parser.rs: parse_js_file uses splitn(2,'=') and trims; parse_i64 added for sizeBytes/favorite/retweet counts; doc comments expanded. Behavior aligns with JS wrapper format and should be more tolerant.
- src/config.rs/logging.rs/error.rs/lib.rs/cli.rs: mostly must_use/const/default/allow annotations and doc tweaks; no functional changes.

Potential concerns (not confirmed bugs):
- Export of DMs lacks conversation_id context (DirectMessage model does not carry it). If CLI intended to export conversations, model/API may need extension.
- Several CLI flags remain unimplemented in main.rs (sort/since/until/replies/context/fields). Not introduced by these diffs, but a correctness/UX gap for later bug hunt.

No immediate regressions found that warrant code changes at this step.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:30:01.076013082-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:34:02.360639254-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:34:02.360646728-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-9rf.4","depends_on_id":"xf-9rf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:30:01.078025102-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"xf-9rf.4","depends_on_id":"xf-9rf.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:30:10.794020969-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"xf-9rf.5","title":"Fresh-eyes bug hunt + correctness fixes","description":"Goal:
- Conduct a careful, methodical bug hunt across the codebase and fix issues with minimal, test‑backed diffs.

Steps:
1) Re‑read critical paths (parser/search/storage/CLI) with fresh eyes.
2) Identify correctness risks: unchecked assumptions, lossy conversions, silent fallbacks, bad error handling.
3) For each issue:
   - Document root cause, impact, and reproduction steps.
   - Add a failing unit/integration test **before** the fix when feasible.
4) Apply minimal fix using `apply_patch`; no unrelated refactors.

Testing & Verification:
- Add unit tests for each fix; add or extend E2E scripts when behavior changes are user‑visible.
- E2E scripts must include detailed logs (command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing).
- Run `cargo check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant tests.

Acceptance:
- All fixes include tests and root‑cause notes.
- No regressions; quality gates recorded.
- Any behavioral changes documented with before/after examples.
","notes":"Fresh-eyes fixes: enforce manifest.js presence (parse_manifest uses required reader + test), enable prefix matching by indexing with default tokenizer and querying text_prefix, add prefix search test, and update cli_e2e fixtures with manifest + JSON validation logging. Ran fmt/check/clippy, cargo test parser/search, UBS --diff.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:34:40.255520526-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T09:58:29.636618276-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T09:58:29.636618276-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: fresh-eyes fixes, tests, quality gates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-9rf.5","depends_on_id":"xf-9rf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:34:40.256783946-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"xf-9rf.5","depends_on_id":"xf-9rf.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:34:50.320708583-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"xf-pkc","title":"Task: Add cache perf/log tests","description":"## Purpose\nTrack remaining cache tests omitted from xf-49 due to determinism concerns.\n\n## Scope\n- Add non-flaky perf smoke checks (optional/ignored test or doc-only benchmark) to compare cold vs warm cache timings.\n- Capture/log cache load timing and loaded_now flag in tests using tracing test subscriber.\n- Add large embedding set test if feasible (bound size, avoid memory blowups).\n\n## Acceptance\n- Tests are deterministic or marked #[ignore] with clear instructions.\n- Logs show cache hit/miss with timing in test output.\n- No new flaky CI failures.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:26:05.188855276-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T05:26:26.041457597-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-pkc","depends_on_id":"xf-49","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:26:05.214609992-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
